look.speaker: player
look.text: My desk. Buried under takeaway containers, unpaid invoices, and a fine layer of self-loathing.

look.speaker: player
look.text: There's a hip flask in the top drawer. It's empty, but it's the principle.

then.speaker: player
then.text: I rummage through the drawer. Past the expired licence, the broken wand, and... hello.

then.speaker: player
then.text: My hip flask. Empty, of course. But having it on me makes me feel like a real detective.

else.speaker: player
else.text: I've already taken everything worth taking from this desk. Which was one empty flask. Grim.

look.speaker: player
look.text: My crystal ball. Grade D standard issue. It gets three channels, two of which are shopping networks.

use.speaker: player
use.text: I wave my hand over the crystal ball. It flickers to life, showing...

use.speaker: player
use.text: ...an advert for 'WizardDirect Insurance.' Your magical malpractice cover, sorted.

use.speaker: player
use.text: I turn it off. Some visions are too horrifying even for me.

then.speaker: player
then.text: The filing cabinet. I've already pulled Gerald's file. Ethel's system actually works, against all odds.

else.speaker: player
else.text: Ethel's filing cabinet. She maintains it with religious fervour. I'm not allowed to touch it without a proper filing reference.

else.speaker: player
else.text: Last time I tried to file something myself, she didn't speak to me for a week. Best week of my career.

then.speaker: player
then.text: I've already got what I need from the cabinet.

then.speaker: player
then.text: I should use the filing reference card on the cabinet. Even I know that much.

else.speaker: player
else.text: I need a filing reference to use this. Ethel's system is sacrosanct. The last person who misfiled something was never seen again.

else.speaker: player
else.text: I assume she fired them. I prefer not to think about the alternatives.

look.speaker: player
look.text: 'Practical Thaumaturgy for the Reluctant Practitioner.' 'So Your Wand Is Broken: Now What?' 'Legal Loopholes in Magical Licensing (3rd Edition).'

look.speaker: player
look.text: All excellent references. All completely unread.

use.speaker: player
use.text: I pull out a book at random. 'Self-Help for the Magically Impaired.' Chapter one: 'Accept That It's Probably Your Fault.'

look.speaker: player
look.text: My hat stand. It's holding a fedora I bought when I thought being a PI would be glamorous.

look.speaker: player
look.text: That was three years, forty-seven cases, and one major existential crisis ago.

use.speaker: player
use.text: I put the fedora on. Then I take it off. I'm not a fedora person. I've accepted this.

look.speaker: player
look.text: 'WANTED: The Magnificent Bastardo. For crimes against magic, good taste, and several load-bearing walls.'

look.speaker: player
look.text: Reward: 5000 gold pieces. I could pay my rent for a year with that. Two years if I stop eating.

look.speaker: player
look.text: A small city of takeaway containers. Kebab boxes mostly, from downstairs. Rashid gives me a discount.

look.speaker: player
look.text: I think he feels sorry for me. Everyone feels sorry for me. It's my most marketable quality.

use.speaker: player
use.text: I check a container. Empty. Like my flask. And my career prospects.

look.speaker: player
look.text: The window overlooks the high street. There's a man arguing with a pigeon. The pigeon appears to be winning.

look.speaker: player
look.text: Enchanted pigeons. Another thing nobody asked for.

then.speaker: player
then.text: Home sweet office. It smells of old kebab and broken dreams. So, the usual.

then.speaker: player
then.text: Right. Better see what this minotaur wants. But first... I should grab a few things.
